mainEntity.name: How do I write FAQ content that actually helps my business show up in local voice search instead of just filling a page?

acceptedAnswer.text:
To make your FAQs truly effective for local voice search, build them from real questions customers ask and answer in direct, conversational sentences. Each answer should be clear enough that an assistant could read it aloud and the listener would have everything they need.

Generic or overly promotional answers often get ignored.

KEY FACTORS
- Use natural wording instead of keyword stuffing.
- Include local details like neighborhoods, hours, and common scenarios.
- Cover canonical topics: what it costs, how it works, how long it takes, and what could go wrong.

Regularly update your FAQs based on phone inquiries, emails, and reviews, so they stay aligned with current concerns. That dynamic relevance helps assistants trust your content.

Think of FAQs as your spoken script library for every local query that matters.
acceptedAnswer.description: Base FAQs on real customer questions and answer each in a short, clear, conversational paragraph that could be read aloud as-is. Include local details—hours, neighborhoods, costs, timelines—and avoid keyword stuffing. Update them regularly as questions evolve.
